text.title: Trunk-Based Challenges & Diagnostics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the trunk-based-challenges methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The diagnostic MUST walk the five checks in order and report the first failing one as the fix priority. Reporting multiple fixes simultaneously is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Teams try to fix everything at once and finish nothing. The first failing check is upstream; fixing it often eliminates downstream symptoms.

rule.statement: Trunk that breaks > 1/week MUST be diagnosed as missing gate (CI gate, branch protection, flag, PR-size discipline) — never as 'trunk-based development is wrong for us'. Recommending reversion to long-lived branches is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Reverting to long-lived branches hides the broken practices; merge conflicts and integration failures resurface later, worse.

rule.statement: Branches longer than 24 hours MUST be justified by ONE of: (a) feature too big — apply task decomposition; (b) no flag infra — implement flags; (c) compliance cut-point — release branch only. Other justifications are forbidden.
rule.rationale: 'This feature requires a long branch' is almost always a proxy for missing decomposition or missing flags; addressing the root cause is cheaper than maintaining exceptions.

rule.statement: PRs MUST be under 200 lines of diff (excluding generated files and fixtures). Review SLA MUST be under 4 hours.
rule.rationale: Review time scales non-linearly with PR size; 500-line PRs take 4x as long and produce 10x more comments. Small PRs flow fast through the gate.

rule.statement: If one person is the only approver for a domain, reviewer rotation MUST be introduced. Single-reviewer bottlenecks produce queues resembling long-lived branch pressure.
rule.rationale: Single approver = single point of failure; queues build, batches grow, gates erode.

rule.statement: Active feature flags MUST be tracked in .aidocs/feature-flags.md (or equivalent) with task id + cleanup ticket. Untracked flags are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Flags accumulate without ownership; tasks show done while features remain undeployed.
